@@ -450,7 +450,7 @@
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.2","title":"Expose watcher health snapshot in CLI (status/health command)","description":"# Task: Expose watcher health snapshot

## Goal
Make the watcher’s health state visible so we can quickly diagnose:
- stuck tailers
- slow polling
- DB backpressure
- pattern match overload

## Metrics (example)
- discovery tick duration
- tailer count + per-domain breakdown
- per-pane last_seen + last_seq
- storage write queue depth
- event bus queue depths
- FTS insert latency

## Deliverables
- `wa status --health` (or `wa health`) prints the snapshot.
- Robot/MCP can access the same snapshot if useful.

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests:
  - health snapshot JSON is schema-parseable and stable
  - “stuck pane” synthetic scenario is surfaced clearly

- CLI contract tests:
  - covered by `wa-nu4.3.2.11` (stable JSON output in non-TTY mode)

- Diagnostic coverage:
  - `wa doctor` tests (`wa-nu4.3.4.8`) should validate that health signals are consistent with watcher state when available.

## Acceptance Criteria
- When a pane stops producing output, health snapshot highlights it clearly.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.3","title":"Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized): logs/config/DB stats/recent events","description":"# Task: Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized)

## Goal
Generate a single artifact users can attach to bug reports that contains:
- environment info (OS, wa version)
- config summary (redacted)
- DB health stats (counts, schema version, WAL size)
- last N events + workflow step logs (redacted)
- **active pane reservations + recent reservation conflicts (redacted)**

## Safety requirements
- Never include secrets.
- Apply PolicyEngine redaction rules to any text fields.
- Prefer summaries over raw transcripts.

## Output
- A directory or archive (e.g. `.tar.zst`) written to a user-specified path.

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests: `wa-nu4.3.4.7` validates:
  - redaction (no secrets)
  - stable file layout
  - stable metadata fields
  - works while watcher is running

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bundle can be generated while watcher is running.
- Bundle contents are stable and documented.


LABELS: area-diagnostics, area-export, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.8.3: Secret redaction for audit logs (tokens, api keys, passwords) + safe logging conventions ● P0
  → ○ wa-4vx.9.2: Implement config loader: defaults + file + env + CLI overrides; show effective config ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4: [EPIC] Diagnostics: wa doctor/status health, debug bundle, performance budgets ● P1
  → ○ wa-4vx.3.6: Retention & hygiene: prune old segments, maintenance log, explicit vacuum command ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.1: [Human command] `wa export` (segments/events/workflows to JSONL/NDJSON with filters) ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.2: DB snapshot export (safe copy, WAL handling, size checks) ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.5: Export audit trail (JSONL): actions, policy decisions, verification summaries ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.6.1: Reservation model + storage schema (pane_reservations) + expiry ● P2

BLOCKS
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.4.6: [Human command] `wa diag bundle` (wrapper around sanitized diagnostic bundle export) ● P2
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.4.7: Diagnostic bundle tests: redaction, file layout, stable metadata (no secrets) ● P2

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.4","title":"Perf budgets + regression checks (ingest tick, pattern match, FTS inserts)","description":"# Task: Perf budgets + regression checks

## Goal
Define and enforce basic performance budgets so wa stays fast as features accrete.

## Key hot paths
- Quick reject / prefiltering (if applicable)
- Delta extraction + seq assignment
- Pattern detection over deltas
- DB writes (segments + FTS)

## Deliverables
- Criterion benches for hot paths (some may already exist; extend to cover end-to-end ingest tick).
- Budget thresholds (warn or fail) suitable for CI.

## Testing / CI expectations
- Use coarse budgets to avoid noisy failures.
- CI should detect gross regressions (order-of-magnitude) reliably and upload benchmark artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CI can detect a gross regression (e.g., 10× slowdown) reliably.
- Benches are stable (avoid noisy wall-clock assertions; use coarse budgets).

-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.5","title":"Metrics endpoint (Prometheus): wa watch --metrics bind + core counters/histograms","description":"# Task: Metrics endpoint (Prometheus)

## Goal
Expose optional Prometheus metrics to support long-running reliability:
- ingest lag
- gaps
- queue depths
- pattern detection latency
- workflow outcomes
- DB write latencies

## Design
- Disabled by default.
- Bind address configurable (default localhost).
- Must not significantly slow down hot path.

## Safety
- Default bind is localhost-only.
- Binding publicly requires an explicit, scary flag (proposed): `--dangerous-bind-any`.
- Metrics must never include transcript contents or secrets.

## Deliverables
- `wa watch --metrics <bind>` and/or config-driven enable.
- A minimal set of stable metric names.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - metric names are stable
  - registry can be instantiated with metrics disabled (zero overhead path)

- Integration tests:
  - start watcher with metrics enabled (bind to localhost ephemeral port)
  - fetch `/metrics` and assert:
    - response is valid Prometheus text format
    - key metrics appear (even if counts are zero)

- Safety tests:
  - default bind is localhost
  - public bind requires `--dangerous-bind-any`
  - enabling metrics does not leak secrets

## Acceptance Criteria
- When enabled, `/metrics` responds with valid Prometheus text format.
- When disabled, zero overhead on hot path.

## Goal

- ingest lag

## Design
- Disabled by default.
- Bind address configurable (default localhost).

## Safety
- Default bind is localhost-only.

## Deliverables

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.6","title":"[Human command] `wa diag bundle` (wrapper around sanitized diagnostic bundle export)","description":"# Task: `wa diag bundle`

## Goal
Provide the CLI surface described in the plan:
- `wa diag bundle --last 15m --output <path>`

This should wrap the diagnostic bundle generator (`wa-nu4.3.4.3`) and ensure the UX is great:
- clear progress logging
- clear output location
- stable exit codes

## Requirements
- Redaction always on by default.
- If requested output path exists, refuse unless `--force`.
- Include enabled features + versions in the bundle.

## Testing
- Covered by `wa-nu4.3.4.7` (bundle tests) and should include:
  - CLI exit codes and refusal behavior (`--force`)
  - stable metadata fields
  - no secrets in stdout/stderr or bundle contents

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running `wa diag bundle` produces a bundle containing:
  - config summary (redacted)
  - versions
  - recent events + workflow logs
  - health snapshot
  - audit excerpts (if enabled)

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.7","title":"Diagnostic bundle tests: redaction, file layout, stable metadata (no secrets)","description":"# Task: Diagnostic bundle tests

## Goal
Ensure diagnostic bundles are reliable and safe.

## Testing
- Bundle layout contains required files.
- Redaction applied (no secrets in outputs).
- Stable metadata present (`version`, `enabled_features`).
- Reservation snapshot present when reservations exist (owner/TTL, redacted).
- If bundle already exists and `--force` not set, command fails safely.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests are deterministic and do not require a running WezTerm instance (use fixture DB and fixture config).


LABELS: area-diagnostics, area-tests, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4: [EPIC] Diagnostics: wa doctor/status health, debug bundle, performance budgets ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4.3: Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized): logs/config/DB stats/recent events ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4.6: [Human command] `wa diag bundle` (wrapper around sanitized diagnostic bundle export) ● P2

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.8","title":"Doctor tests: fixture-based checks + output stability (JSON/TTY)","description":"# Task: `wa doctor` tests

## Goal
Make `wa doctor` a **trustworthy** and **regression-resistant** diagnostic surface.

`wa doctor` is how users decide whether to trust the tool. If it flakes, lies, or leaks secrets, we lose users.

## Test scope

### Unit tests
- Path resolution rendering (workspace root, DB/log/lock paths) is correct and deterministic.
- JSON output shape is stable (fields present, naming stable, no secret leakage).
- Redaction is applied to any potentially sensitive field (even on error paths).

### Integration tests (fixture-driven; no real WezTerm required)
Run `wa doctor` in a hermetic test environment that simulates:
- A healthy system.
- Missing `wezterm` binary / `wezterm cli list` failure.
- Workspace not writable.
- DB unreadable / schema mismatch.
- Watcher not running / lock held / stale lock.
- Optional feature checks present/absent (mcp/browser/web/tui) without forcing those deps.

## Test strategy / harness design
- Prefer dependency injection where feasible (traits) so we can simulate:
  - `wezterm` CLI calls
  - storage open/migration version
  - watcher health snapshot
- If process-based stubbing is simpler, use a test harness that:
  - writes a small “fake wezterm” executable to a temp dir
  - prepends that temp dir to `PATH`
  - feeds deterministic stdout/stderr for specific subcommands

## Output stability requirements
- Non-TTY mode:
  - `wa doctor --format json` must be strictly machine-parseable and stable.
  - Tests validate JSON against a versioned schema (if/when we add a schema for doctor output).
- TTY mode:
  - Tests validate key content (not exact ANSI art); either strip ANSI or snapshot a normalized rendering.

## Logging & debuggability
- Tests must capture logs on failure and print them (bounded) to make failures diagnosable.
- Ensure logs do not leak secrets (redaction applied to any dynamic content).

## Deliverables
- Unit test module(s) for doctor output + redaction.
- Integration test harness utilities for stubbing wezterm/storage/watcher.
- A small set of golden fixtures covering the major failure modes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa doctor` has coverage for each major check and for each major failure mode.
- Tests do not require:
  - network access
  - a real WezTerm instance
  - a real user home directory
- Adding a new doctor check requires adding at least one test covering both success and failure paths.

## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Ensure the integration harness actually uses the fake `wezterm` binary (assert invocation).
  - Add an explicit assertion that `--format json` output contains no ANSI escapes and validates against schema (once added).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GrayRidge","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T12:17:30.311960939Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-07T04:18:16.728019600Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T04:18:16.727950151Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-diagnostics","area-tests","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.8","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.8","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.8","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"wa-upg.1.3","title":"Human command: wa reproduce (export last incident bundle)","description":"# Task: Human command `wa reproduce`

## Goal
Provide an ergonomic human-facing command that produces an incident bundle with minimal effort.

## Requirements
- CLI behavior:
  - default: export "most relevant recent incident" bundle
  - flags:
    - `--since <duration>`
    - `--kind <crash|policy|workflow|gap|manual>`
    - `--out <path>`
    - `--include-db-slice` (dangerous-ish; still sanitized)
    - `--include-transcript` (explicit opt-in)
  - prints a clear summary of contents and next steps

- Output format:
  - TTY: friendly summary with paths
  - `--format json`: machine-readable output containing:
    - bundle path
    - kind
    - manifest version
    - suggested replay commands

- Safety:
  - warn and require explicit flag for higher-risk inclusions

## Testing
- CLI contract tests:
  - `--format json` validates schema and stable keys
  - error cases are actionable and suggest `wa doctor`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa reproduce` creates a bundle and prints actionable next steps.
- `--format json` output is stable enough for scripts.

 {"id":"wa-upg.1.4","title":"Human command: wa reproduce replay (deterministic incident replayer)","description":"# Task: `wa reproduce replay`

## Goal
Make incident bundles actionable by providing deterministic replay tooling that reproduces key outcomes from the bundle.

## Requirements
- Command: `wa reproduce replay <bundle>`
- Supported replay modes (initial):
  - `policy`: re-run PolicyEngine evaluation on recorded decision context and assert the same result
  - `rules`: re-run rule/pattern engine on recorded segments and assert expected detections
- Output:
  - TTY: readable summary of pass/fail with pointers to traces
  - `--format json`: structured results (per-check) with stable error codes
- Failure handling:
  - if replay cannot be performed (missing data), print a clear explanation and "what to do next".

## Testing
- Unit tests for:
  - bundle load + manifest validation
  - replay determinism on fixtures
- Integration tests:
  - golden bundles in `tests/fixtures/incident_bundles/*`

## Acceptance Criteria
- A valid bundle can be replayed and yields deterministic outputs.
- Replay failures are actionable and do not leak secrets.

 {"id":"wa-upg.1.5","title":"Tests: incident bundle + replay (unit/integration fixtures)","description":"# Task: Tests for incident bundle + replay

## Goal
Make incident bundling/replay trustworthy by adding comprehensive unit + integration tests.

## Requirements
- Add deterministic fixtures:
  - fixture DB with a small set of segments/events
  - fixture logs with known secret tokens to verify redaction
  - fixture decision trace(s)
- Test categories:
  - bundle building (layout + manifest)
  - privacy budget enforcement
  - redaction correctness (no secrets anywhere)
  - replay correctness (policy + rules modes)

## Logging/artifacts
- On test failure, print paths to the generated bundle directory and minimal diff hints.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover the main success paths and the important failure modes (missing data, invalid schema, over-budget truncation).
- Failures are actionable and do not require manual digging.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CalmLynx","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:35:30.457964683Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-06T03:00:48.581672444Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T03:00:48.581531192Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-diagnostics","area-tests","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.1.5","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:44Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"wa-upg.1.6","title":"E2E: incident bundle export + replay (verbose logs + artifacts)","description":"# Task: E2E incident bundle export + replay

## Goal
Add an end-to-end scenario that proves incident bundle export and replay works against a real-ish runtime.

## Scenario
- Start a watcher in a controlled test workspace.
- Produce a deterministic incident:
  - example: trigger a known policy denial (AltScreen or recent GAP) OR a known rule match.
- Run `wa reproduce` to export a bundle.
- Run `wa reproduce replay` and assert the expected replay result.

## Requirements
- No fixed sleeps. Use wait-for/quiescence utilities with bounded timeouts.
- Verbose logs:
  - command transcript
  - watcher logs (captured)
  - paths to artifacts
- Artifacts:
  - the generated bundle
  - a small "summary.json" with key assertions

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E is deterministic and debuggable.
- On failure, artifacts are sufficient to reproduce locally.

 {"id":"wa-upg.1.7","title":"Docs: incident bundles + wa reproduce (shareable support workflow)","description":"# Task: Document incident bundles and `wa reproduce`

## Goal
Make incident bundles discoverable and usable by humans and by support processes.

## Requirements
- Document:
  - when to run `wa reproduce`
  - what data is included by default (and what is not)
  - privacy implications + opt-in flags
  - how to replay a bundle
  - how to attach to a GitHub issue (or share internally)
- Include examples of:
  - a policy denial bundle
  - a rule replay bundle
  - a watcher crash bundle

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new contributor can follow docs to generate and replay a bundle without guesswork.

 {"id":"wa-upg.10","title":"[EPIC] Schema-driven docs + client generation (robot/MCP as real API)","description":"# [EPIC] Schema-driven docs + client generation

## Mission
Treat wa's robot/MCP interfaces as a real API:
- schemas are authoritative
- docs are generated from schemas
- typed clients can be generated or maintained with confidence

## Why This Matters
If an integration breaks silently, users lose trust.
Schemas + generated docs/types provide:
- contract stability
- faster integration development
- safer refactors

## Scope
- Build on the existing JSON Schema work:
  - versioned schemas for robot outputs and error objects
- Add:
  - schema-driven docs pages (render schemas into readable reference)
  - optional client generation (at least one language) OR type-safe Rust client crate
  - CI checks to ensure schema/doc generation stays in sync

## Success Criteria
- Another tool can generate types and call wa robot/MCP reliably.
- Schema changes are intentional and reviewed.

## Testing
- Schema validations in CI.
- Golden tests for generated docs output stability.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Docs reflect schemas automatically.
- At least one typed client path exists and is tested.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:48:35.314788392Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:23:16.057643541Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:23:16.057503701Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-docs","area-mcp","area-robot","area-tests","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.10","depends_on_id":"wa-upg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:44Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.10.1","title":"Design: schema→docs/types strategy (direction, versioning, languages)","description":"# Task: Decide schema-driven generation strategy

## Goal
Choose the practical strategy for schema-driven docs and typed clients.

## Requirements
- Decide generation direction:
  - Rust structs → JSON Schema (preferred for single source of truth)
  - OR JSON Schema → types (and keep schemas authored by hand)
- Decide client target(s):
  - TypeScript (common for tooling)
  - Python (common for scripts)
  - Rust client crate (for in-repo safety)
- Decide versioning policy:
  - how schema versions map to wa versions
  - how breaking changes are detected

## Acceptance Criteria
- Strategy is chosen and consistent with repo goals (robust, low tech debt).
","notes":"Decision documented in PLAN.md section 21.5; ready to close once parent unblocked.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GrayRidge","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:48:47.647300556Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:19:50.945432357Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:19:50.945306944Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-design","area-docs","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.10.1","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:44Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":226,"issue_id":"wa-upg.10.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Strategy decided: Rust structs → JSON Schema via schemars (single source of truth). Implemented ApiVersion (pre/post-1.0 compat), SchemaRegistry (24 endpoints), ChangeKind classification, SchemaDiffResult. 27 tests. Commit f15fcb4.","created_at":"2026-02-06T18:53:37Z"}]}
